headline: New vanadium flow battery player Eora Energy launches in Australia with mining and date centre focus
summary_80w: Eora Energy has officially launched in Australia and is positioning itself as a challenger in the vanadium redox flow battery market.

headline: European Energy divests 470MW Lithuania hybrid project to Energix
summary_80w: Danish independent power producer (IPP) European Energy has divested a 470MW hybrid project in Lithuania to Israel-based IPP Energix.

headline: Inox Clean Energy adds 1.3GW capacity in Vibrant Energy buyout
summary_80w: Indian independent power producer (IPP) Inox Clean Energy has acquired the Macquarie-owned Vibrant Energy, which operates a 1,337MW commercial and industrial-focused renewables portfolio across India.

headline: EU grid constraints put 120GW of renewables at risk
summary_80w: In its analysis, Ember examined grid capacity across 20 EU countries and found the major gap was at the transmission level, with a possible shortfall of 104 GW that would affect utility-scale solar projects.

headline: Japan’s first capacity market-contracted BESS brought online by Hexa Energy
summary_80w: Developer-investor Hexa Energy Services has inaugurated what is thought to be Japan’s first operational battery storage project with a capacity market contract.

headline: IRENA: World adds 510GW of new solar PV capacity in 2025
summary_80w: The world added 510GW of new solar PV capacity in 2025, the most of any electricity generation source, according to IRENA.

headline: Solar saving EU ‘millions a day’ since outbreak of Middle East war
summary_80w: Solar power has saved the EU over €110 million (US$127.5 million) a day since the outbreak of war in the Middle East, according to SolarPower Europe.

headline: Japan’s Toyo hits solar cell and module shipment targets, posts 142% revenue increase
summary_80w: Toyo Solar shipped 4.5GW of cells in FY2025, surpassing its full-year target, while module shipments reached 249MW.

headline: European-made solar arrays to power first crewed mission to the Moon since 1972
summary_80w: Four giant solar ‘wings’ will provide power for the first crewed mission to the Moon in over 50 years, due to launch later today.

headline: Pexapark: Middle East conflict could increase European solar PPA prices by 35%
summary_80w: The conflict in the Middle East could drive European solar PPA prices up by as much as 35%, according to Pexapark.

headline: Green steel, data centres and hydrogen push South Australia’s electricity demand toward 50TWh by 2040
summary_80w: South Australia could see its peak load double from 3.3GW today to 6.5-7GW by 2040, driven by data centres, green steel and hydrogen demand.

headline: VIDEO – Energy Storage Summit 2026: What does life after MACSE look like in Italy?
summary_80w: What is the future for the grid-scale energy storage market in Italy, now that the dust has settled from the country's long-awaited MACSE auction for BESS?

headline: EnerVenue closes US$300 million Series B for 30,000-cycle nickel-hydrogen battery manufacturing

headline: Harmony starts building third 100MW/200MWh French BESS

headline: UL9540A: Shift to system-level testing defines new edition of key BESS safety standard
summary_80w: ESN Premium discusses the sixth edition of the key industry safety standard, UL9540A, with Dana Parmenter of the standards organisation and NRTL, CSA Group.

headline: ‘We learned more in the past six months than the past six years’: Australian battery storage lenders navigate merchant risk

headline: Abu Dhabi launches residential solar deployment policy

headline: Michigan approves 1,332MW of BESS with 332MW supporting Oracle data centre

headline: JV August Mega Energy looks to expand Vietnam’s C&I solar sector

headline: GameChange Solar earthquake tracker testing yields no ‘meaningful power loss’ in most trials

headline: 100-hour LDES battery technologies from Form, Noon and Ore: how do they compare?

summary_80w: In this news roundup, Unigrid, Inlyte Energy, CIUDEN, and Sunamp are advancing non-lithium energy storage technologies worldwide.
